_comment: Curated illustrative examples for the regulatory-reporting use-case core concepts. Real-world supervisory/filing instances (general domain knowledge), not FIBO axioms. Applied by to_okf when FIBO supplies none; provenance curated.

examples.title: regulatory agency
examples: The US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) or the UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA).

examples.title: primary federal regulator
examples: The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) as the primary federal regulator of a national bank.

examples.title: self-regulating organization
examples: FINRA, which regulates US broker-dealers under SEC oversight.

examples.title: fund supervisory authority
examples: The authority that authorizes and supervises a UCITS fund, such as the CSSF in Luxembourg.

examples.title: government agency
examples: A government agency such as the US Treasury's FinCEN.

examples.title: government body
examples: A central bank such as the Federal Reserve.

examples.title: business registration authority
examples: A companies registry such as the UK Companies House.

examples.title: regulated exchange
examples: The New York Stock Exchange, a regulated securities exchange.

examples.title: regulatory capacity
examples: The capacity in which the SEC acts when it supervises a registered broker-dealer.

examples.title: regulatory service
examples: A supervisory service provided by a regulator, such as reviewing registration filings.

examples.title: regulation
examples: Regulation Z (Truth in Lending), issued under US federal law.

examples.title: regulation identifier
examples: 'Regulation Z', the identifier for the US Truth in Lending regulation.

examples.title: regulation identification scheme
examples: The 'Regulation A/B/C…' labelling scheme used to identify US Federal Reserve regulations.

examples.title: financial markets regulation
examples: MiFID II, which governs the conduct of EU financial markets.

examples.title: consumer credit protection law
examples: The US Consumer Credit Protection Act.

examples.title: reg b
examples: Regulation B, implementing the US Equal Credit Opportunity Act.

examples.title: reg z
examples: Regulation Z, implementing the US Truth in Lending Act.

examples.title: legal obligation
examples: A covered lender's legal obligation to file a HMDA report each year.

examples.title: report
examples: An annual report filed by a company with its regulator.

examples.title: regulatory report
examples: A HMDA loan/application register filed with the CFPB, or a MiFID II transaction report.

examples.title: rating report
examples: A credit rating agency's report assigning an issuer an 'AA' rating.

examples.title: credit report
examples: A consumer credit report from Experian showing an individual's credit history.

examples.title: credit report product
examples: A tri-merge credit report product combining Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion data.

examples.title: remittance report
examples: A monthly servicer remittance report on payments collected for a mortgage pool.

examples.title: HMDA report
examples: A bank's annual Home Mortgage Disclosure Act Loan/Application Register filed with the CFPB.

examples.title: income verification report
examples: A report verifying a borrower's income from pay stubs and tax returns during underwriting.

examples.title: disclosure provision
examples: A loan-agreement clause requiring the APR to be disclosed to the borrower.

examples.title: disclosure requirement
examples: The Truth in Lending requirement to disclose the APR and finance charge.

examples.title: product disclosure requirement
examples: A requirement to provide a Key Facts document for a mortgage product.

examples.title: borrower disclosure requirement
examples: The requirement to give a borrower a Loan Estimate within three business days of application.

examples.title: loan regulatory requirement
examples: The requirement that a covered lender report HMDA data on its mortgage originations.

examples.title: consumer credit requirement
examples: A requirement that consumer-credit advertising state the representative APR.

examples.title: data protection requirement
examples: A requirement to protect borrower personal data under data-protection law such as GDPR.

examples.title: reporting party
examples: The bank that files a HMDA report is the reporting party.

examples.title: trade reporting facility
examples: A FINRA Trade Reporting Facility (TRF) that captures off-exchange US equity trades.

examples.title: approved reporting mechanism
examples: A MiFID II Approved Reporting Mechanism (ARM) that submits transaction reports to regulators.

examples.title: data reporting services provider
examples: A MiFID II data reporting services provider operating an ARM or APA.

examples.title: reporting accountant
examples: The accountant who prepares and signs off the financial statements in a regulatory filing.

examples.title: securities trades reporting
examples: Reporting an executed equity trade to a FINRA Trade Reporting Facility within seconds of execution.

examples.title: securities trade date position reporting
examples: Reporting a fund's securities positions as of the trade date for regulatory transparency.

examples.title: register with regulatory authority
examples: Filing a registration statement with the SEC before an initial public offering.

examples.title: filing details
examples: The particulars of a filing: the form type, the filing date, and the receiving authority.

examples.title: business register identifier
examples: A company registration number issued by a national business registry.

examples.title: legal entity identifier
examples: 5493001KJTIIGC8Y1R12, the LEI used to identify a reporting entity (ISO 17442).

examples.title: legal entity identifier registry
examples: A GLEIF-accredited Local Operating Unit that issues and maintains LEIs.

examples.title: universal loan identifier
examples: The Universal Loan Identifier (ULI) assigned to a mortgage for HMDA reporting.

examples.title: Nationwide Mortgage Licensing System and Registry Identifier
examples: The unique identifier a mortgage loan originator holds in the NMLS registry.

examples.title: regulatory action
examples: A regulator's enforcement action fining a firm for a reporting breach.

examples.title: disclosure action
examples: A company's disclosure of a material event to the market.

examples.title: credit rating agency
examples: Moody's or S&P Global Ratings.

examples.title: rating agency
examples: A rating agency such as Fitch Ratings.

examples.title: HMDA covered loan contract
examples: A closed-end home-purchase mortgage that must be reported under HMDA.
